title: coupon — retention windows
description: Coupon data is financial-audit-adjacent. Every redemption is a compliance-relevant financial event — a discount that reduced the revenue recognised on an invoice line. Default retention 7 years (SOX + SOC 2 CC6.3 + national tax law baselines: US, EU, UK, CA). Enterprise tier extends to 10y via `coupon_extended_retention`. TenantErased cascades FK CASCADE hard-delete of every owned row EXCEPT invoice-linked redemptions — those migrate to a compliance archive for legal-obligation retention.
compliance_regimes_referenced: gdpr Art. 5(1)(e) — storage limitation (retention windows justified by tax + fraud audit)
gdpr Art. 17 — right to erasure (with financial-retention carve-out)
gdpr Art. 6(1)(b) — lawful basis: contract (coupon = contractual discount offer)
can-spam Act — promotional coupon emails carry unsubscribe path
us tax law — 7-year financial-record retention (discount ledger for revenue-recognition audit)
sox 404 — internal controls over financial reporting (discount trail retained)
iso 27001 Annex A.12.3 — audit log retention
ccpa 1798.100 — right to know + right to delete (with retention carve-out)

purge.trigger: > 7 (or 10) years past valid_until (when set) OR past deactivated_at + zero non-reversed redemption references
purge.notes: Every redemption traces back to a coupon. Coupon cannot be hard-deleted while any non-reversed redemption retains a reference — retention aligns with the longest-lived downstream (redemptions).

hot.description: Active + first year for dispute resolution

purge.action: hard delete OR migrate to compliance_archive.coupon_redemptions (invoice-linked redemptions)
purge.notes: Redemptions linked to invoices that survive tenant deletion migrate to compliance_archive rather than delete — the invoice financial record requires the discount ledger to persist. Standalone redemptions (targeting membership/transaction without a persistent invoice) may be hard-deleted at the retention boundary.

purge.action: cascaded (JSONB column on the redemption row)
purge.notes: The snapshot is IMMUTABLE — no in-flight edits ever. Under GDPR right-to-erasure, no PII lives in the snapshot (it holds discount config only — currency, amount, applicability, plan_ids).

special_paths.action: FK CASCADE hard-deletes every coupons / coupon_redemptions row.
special_paths.carve_out: Invoice-linked redemptions (applied_to_type='invoice' AND the parent invoice still exists after tenant erasure) migrate to compliance_archive.coupon_redemptions prior to cascade. Audit trail rows survive per audit module design (outlives-source).

special_paths.action: RedactCouponRedemptionsOnUserErasure hook: schedule a 90-day-post-erasure redaction of customer_id + customer_type on redemptions. Row survives for the 7-year audit trail; the customer identity is redacted.
special_paths.carve_out: Retention windows on the redemption row unchanged — financial retention overrides erasure per legal-obligation carve-out.

special_paths.action: Retention windows do NOT reset — deactivation is a business-lifecycle event, not a retention-lifecycle event. The 7 (or 10) year window continues to run from valid_until (or deactivated_at when valid_until is null).
reversed_at is set; the row remains in place. Retention window continues to run from redeemed_at (the original event) — reversal does not reset the clock.

cross_reference: audit::retention.json — audit rows outlive their source rows regardless (financial audit + tax audit trail).
activity::retention.json — activity rows follow their own tier-based retention.
finance::invoice retention (Wave 4) — invoice-linked redemptions align with invoice retention windows.
growth::referrals retention — referral-issued coupons align with referrals retention (7y default / 10y Enterprise).
